actual_meaning: buddy / pal; also a filler interjection like 'man' or 'dude'
note: Hindi has 'dost' for the noun, but as a vocative filler ('arre yaar') it carries an emotional tone no single Tamil word matches; usually kept verbatim.

actual_meaning: bro / mate; affectionate or street address to a male peer
note: Tamil 'thambi/anna' approximates by relative age; the peer-equal 'bhai' vibe is closest to Tamil 'machan'.

actual_meaning: respectful address to an older man, shopkeeper, auto driver, or stranger
note: Tamil 'anna' maps well; the North-Indian flavour (and class connotations) is partly lost.

actual_meaning: casual address to a male friend; Gen-Z filler
note: English loanword used identically in Hindi and Tamil youth speech; needs no translation.

actual_meaning: affectionate address to a younger person of either gender; also condescending 'kiddo'
note: Tamil 'kanna/thangam'; the patronising 'listen here, beta' tone needs a register match, not a literal word.

actual_meaning: affectionate address to a girl/young woman
note: Tamil 'ma/kanna'; gendered and warm, straightforward to localise.

actual_meaning: friendly/cheeky address to any man, often a stranger or vendor; not literal employer
note: The mock-deferential 'kya boss' use is idiomatic; Tamil keeps 'boss' too but the playful tone must be re-acted, not translated.

actual_meaning: admiring address to a skilled or clever person; 'maestro'
note: Literal sense exists in both languages, but the slangy 'kya guru' admiration is colloquial Hindi; Tamil would use a different riff.

actual_meaning: expert; admiring or teasing address to someone clever or skilled
note: Carries Urdu/Hindustani musical-craft connotations; Tamil has no equivalent loaded term, so adapt.

actual_meaning: jocular respectful address to a man, like 'boss' or 'captain'
note: English loanword reused in both target languages; mostly carries over.

actual_meaning: an older student/colleague one defers to; college and workplace hierarchy term
note: Indian-English institutional usage; both languages borrow the word but the strong deference culture is contextual.

actual_meaning: a younger student/colleague; also affectionate for a small kid
note: English loanword used identically; near-transparent.

actual_meaning: buddy / mate; Mumbai street address for a friend
note: Iconic Bambaiya-Hindi (Dada Kondke / Bollywood tapori) slang; no Tamil cognate, must be re-localised to Tamil street talk.

actual_meaning: dude / bro; ubiquitous Tamil male-peer address
note: Core Tamil slang; literally 'brother-in-law' shortened. Into Hindi it must become 'bhai/yaar' — direct keep would confuse Hindi audiences.

actual_meaning: close male friend / bro; kinship word repurposed as buddy slang
note: Tamil source term; the brother-in-law-to-buddy semantic shift has no Hindi parallel, so adapt to 'bhai/yaar'.

actual_meaning: casual masculine 'man/dude' tag appended to address a male equal or junior
note: Tamil grammatical particle of familiarity ('enna da'); cannot be translated word-for-word, only its informal register can be matched.

actual_meaning: casual feminine counterpart of 'da'; addressing a female equal/junior informally
note: Gendered Tamil familiarity particle; untranslatable as a word, localise via register.

actual_meaning: one's closest friend; also a gushy term of endearment
note: English loanword used across Hindi and Tamil youth/social-media speech; carries over cleanly.

actual_meaning: a resourceful person who improvises clever workaround fixes
note: Built on the untranslatable 'jugaad'; the can-do hack-it cultural concept must be explained or adapted, not translated.

actual_meaning: a sycophant / yes-man who flatters a powerful person
note: Metaphorical 'spoon'; Tamil and Hindi each have their own sycophant slang, so adapt not translate.

actual_meaning: sycophancy / bootlicking as an activity
note: Noun form of chamcha with the '-giri' productive suffix; concept translatable but the idiom is Hindi-specific.

actual_meaning: a fawning bootlicker; obsequious flatterer
note: Has straightforward equivalents (Tamil 'oorpadi/musukku'); the meaning carries, the exact flavour varies.

actual_meaning: a tacky, try-hard, low-class show-off (derogatory youth slang)
note: Very recent North-Indian internet slang with strong class disdain; no Tamil equivalent, must be re-localised.

actual_meaning: traditional, virtuous and 'family-values' proper; often ironic
note: Rooted in 'sanskaar' (Hindu cultural upbringing); the ironic 'too-wholesome' usage and cultural baggage resist direct Tamil translation.

actual_meaning: a frumpy, unfashionable, conservative woman (mildly derogatory)
note: Literal politeness flips to mockery in slang; the stereotype is North-Indian, needs adaptation in Tamil.

actual_meaning: a woman who shed a frumpy look for a trendy one (BTM)
note: Hyper-specific Delhi-college coinage; only the situation, not the phrase, can be conveyed.

actual_meaning: polite address to any older man; also teasing for someone acting old/uncool
note: English 'uncle' + Hindi honorific; Tamil uses 'mama/anna'. The 'ok uncle' dismissive jab needs tone matching.

actual_meaning: polite address to any older woman; also a 'nosy middle-aged woman' stereotype
note: Pan-Indian English address; Tamil 'aunty/maami'. The judgy-aunty stereotype is cultural shorthand.

actual_meaning: extra-polite address to an older woman
note: English + Hindi honorific blend; Tamil would use 'maami'.

actual_meaning: a blind political/ideological follower (modern derogatory)
note: Religious 'devotee' weaponised into political slang post-2014; literal Tamil 'bhaktan' exists but the political sneer is Hindi-internet-specific.

actual_meaning: a fanatically uncritical follower of a leader/ideology
note: Intensified 'bhakt'; the compound and its political charge don't transfer cleanly to Tamil.

actual_meaning: a man who is excessively submissive/attentive to someone he desires
note: Global internet loanword used as-is in Hindi and Tamil online speech.

actual_meaning: a lecherous, sleazy, sex-obsessed man
note: Tamil 'soothu paaru' types/'thevidiya payyan' register; the meaning maps, the exact slur differs.

actual_meaning: an attractive person (objectifying, usually of a woman); also a 'character/troublemaker'
note: Bollywood-driven objectifying slang ('item number'); Tamil borrows 'item' too but the dual 'attractive/character' sense needs context.

actual_meaning: an attractive person (objectifying); also slang for drugs/contraband
note: Crude metaphor of person-as-merchandise; both target languages have own crude equivalents, adapt not translate.

literal: to make fall / to win over
actual_meaning: to woo / flirt someone into liking you; to charm and seduce
note: The 'make-someone-fall-for-you' idiom; Tamil 'love panrathu/set aaganum' captures it differently, so adapt.

actual_meaning: to flirt / make romantic advances
note: 'Line' = a flirtatious move; Tamil 'line adi' is the exact calque, but into other languages it must be explained.

actual_meaning: a romantic arrangement/fling; also a fixed under-the-table deal
note: Indian-English slang with two senses (romance / bribe-fixing); Tamil 'setting aagiduchu' mirrors the romance sense but tone is context-bound.

actual_meaning: a love affair / illicit liaison; also a confusing entanglement
note: 'Affair' sense ('uska chakkar chal raha hai') is idiomatic; Tamil uses different terms ('lavvu/affair'), so adapt.

actual_meaning: a romantic lover / smitten suitor, often theatrically devoted
note: Urdu/Hindustani poetic register; Tamil 'kaadhalan' is plainer, losing the lyrical Urdu flavour.

literal: the state of being a lover
actual_meaning: romance / the act of being in love (often filmi)
note: Noun of aashiq; Tamil 'kaadhal' covers it but the filmi-Urdu glamour is partly lost.

actual_meaning: a heartbroken man who drowns his sorrows, esp. in drink
note: Allusion to Sarat Chandra's/Bollywood's Devdas; Tamil cinema has its own tragic-lover icons, so substitute the reference.

actual_meaning: a disloyal lover / cheater; one who betrays in love
note: Urdu loanword common in film songs; Tamil 'dhrogi/manaivi mosam' conveys betrayal but not the song-lyric tenderness.

actual_meaning: friend / companion
note: Tamil 'nanban', Hindi 'dost' — universal concept, fully translatable.

actual_meaning: a childhood friend known since infancy; oldest mate
note: 'Langot' (infant's loincloth) marks a friend-since-diapers; Tamil 'siru vayasu nanban' conveys the idea, but the vivid idiom is Hindi.

actual_meaning: one's close friend group / crew
note: English loanword used identically in both languages' youth speech.

actual_meaning: attitude, flashy style, one-upmanship swagger
note: Punjabi-flavoured Hindi slang for cocky style; Tamil 'style/figure' captures part, but the specific swagger needs adaptation.

actual_meaning: bullying, throwing one's weight around, strong-arm intimidation
note: 'Dada' (don/strongman) + '-giri'; Tamil 'rowdyism/aatam' conveys the bullying but the compound is Hindi-specific.

actual_meaning: playing politics; self-serving leader-like grandstanding (derogatory)
note: 'Neta' (politician) + '-giri'; the cynical connotation translates but the coinage is Hindi.

actual_meaning: hooliganism / lawless thuggish behaviour
note: 'Gunda' + '-gardi'; Tamil 'rowdyism' is the everyday equivalent, so adapt.

actual_meaning: a (often petty) government clerk/official; also a term of endearment for a child or partner
note: Dual sense (bureaucrat vs sweetie 'babu') is context-bound; Tamil has no single word covering both.

actual_meaning: affectionate/old-world address to a man, famously filmi
note: Bengali address popularised by 'Anand'; a cultural-film reference rather than a translatable phrase.

actual_meaning: sir; deferential address to a man of authority or a 'sahib' type
note: Colonial-era honorific; Tamil 'saar/aiyaa'. The class-deference flavour partly localises.

actual_meaning: sir / boss; respectful address (Marathi/Gujarati flavour)
note: Regional variant of sahab; same deferential function, Tamil 'saar'.

actual_meaning: respectful address to a woman of authority; also teasing 'madam-ji'
note: Pan-Indian English honorific used identically in both target languages.

actual_meaning: extra-deferential or jokey address to a man
note: English 'sir' + Hindi '-ji'; Tamil 'saar' carries the respect, the doubled honorific is Hindi flavour.

actual_meaning: respectful/affectionate address to an older girl or woman
note: Tamil 'akka' is the direct counterpart; near-perfect map across the two.

actual_meaning: respectful address to an older man / elder brother
note: Tamil source term equating to Hindi 'bhaiya'; transfers cleanly between the two target languages.

actual_meaning: respectful address to an older woman / elder sister
note: Tamil counterpart of Hindi 'didi'; clean kinship-address map.

actual_meaning: affectionate address to a younger man / little brother
note: Tamil source term; Hindi 'chhotu/bhai'. Kinship-address, easy to localise by age.

actual_meaning: affectionate address to a younger girl / little sister
note: Tamil source term; maps to Hindi diminutive address for a young girl.

actual_meaning: address to an older man (uncle); also casual peer slang in some Tamil youth speech
note: Tamil kinship word also used as friendly street address; the slangy peer sense doesn't map to Hindi 'mama' (which is strictly the uncle).

actual_meaning: respectful/casual address to an older or peer man (Tamil)
note: Spelling variant of Tamil mama; carries the same kinship-to-slang extension absent in Hindi.

actual_meaning: address to an older woman; also a Tam-Brahm 'maami' stereotype
note: Tamil kinship address with a community-specific stereotype layer that has no Hindi equivalent.

actual_meaning: darling / dear one; deeply affectionate address to a child or loved one
note: Tamil endearment literally 'eye/pupil'; Hindi 'jaan/laadla' conveys the warmth but not the eye metaphor.

actual_meaning: beloved / precious one; tender term of endearment
note: Poetic Tamil endearment; Hindi has its own ('mehbooba/jaan'), so substitute rather than translate.

actual_meaning: dude / bro; extremely common Tamil male-peer address
note: Core Tamil buddy slang from 'brother-in-law'; into Hindi becomes 'bhai/yaar', not a literal carry.

actual_meaning: oi! / hey you! — abrupt, familiar or rude call to a male
note: Tamil attention-grabbing particle, tone ranges from chummy to insulting; only register, not the word, can be matched.

actual_meaning: informal/cheeky address or dismissal to a girl/woman
note: Gendered Tamil particle; carries playful-to-rude tone untranslatable as a word.

actual_meaning: sir; respectful address to a man
note: South-Indian rendering of 'sir'; the accent itself is a marker, but meaning transfers to Hindi 'sahab/sir'.

actual_meaning: respectful address to an elder or superior man
note: Tamil honorific; Hindi 'sahab/ji'. Respect transfers, the regional warmth is Tamil-specific.

actual_meaning: respectful address to an older woman; also exclamation of pain/surprise
note: Kinship address; Hindi 'maaji/amma'. The interjection use ('ammaa!') needs tone, not literal, transfer.

actual_meaning: respectful/affectionate address to an older man or one's father
note: Tamil kinship address; Hindi 'baba/papa'. Straightforward to localise.

actual_meaning: address to an older man; affectionate 'uncle'
note: Specific Hindi kinship term; Tamil 'chithappa/mama'. Exact relation differs, function maps.

actual_meaning: address to an older man, esp. in Haryana/North; rustic 'uncle'
note: Region-specific Hindi kinship term with rural connotations; Tamil collapses to 'mama/periappa'.

actual_meaning: address to an older woman; auntie
note: Hindi kinship; Tamil 'chithi/periamma'. Specific relation, general 'auntie' function.

actual_meaning: address to an elder brother's wife; also teasing for a friend's girlfriend
note: Tamil 'anni'; the 'friend's-girlfriend' teasing extension is shared culturally but phrased differently.

actual_meaning: sister-in-law; address to an older brother's wife
note: Tamil counterpart of Hindi 'bhabhi'; clean kinship map between the two.

actual_meaning: affectionate address to an elder sister's husband
note: Tamil 'athimber/maama'; relation maps, the playful Hindi diminutive flavour is local.

actual_meaning: a mild abuse/insult ('bastard-ish'); also literal brother-in-law
note: Kinship word doubling as a swear; the insult sense is Hindi-specific and must be matched to a Tamil mild oath.

actual_meaning: son-in-law; also 'pampered guest' stereotype ('damaad jaisa aaram')
note: Tamil 'marumagan'; the 'treated-like-a-prince son-in-law' trope is shared but idiomized differently.

actual_meaning: a favourite, doted-on child or person
note: Tamil 'selladhu/kondaattam'; warm meaning transfers, exact word differs.

actual_meaning: a follower / protege / sidekick
note: Guru-chela pairing; Tamil 'sIdan/sishyan'. Concept transfers, the guru-system flavour is shared Indic.

actual_meaning: a secondary helper companion to a hero/lead
note: English loanword used across both languages; transparent.

actual_meaning: a gullible target / fall guy who gets fooled or scammed
note: 'Goat-for-slaughter' metaphor for a dupe; Tamil 'aattu kutty/ezhavu' differ, so adapt the metaphor.

actual_meaning: a girl, esp. a girlfriend (casual, slightly crude)
note: North-Indian casual slang for a girl/girlfriend; Tamil 'figure/ponnu' fill the slot differently.

actual_meaning: a dude / a guy; generic male person, casual
note: Hindi everyday slang for 'a guy'; Tamil 'aalu/payyan'. Common but idiomatic, adapt.

actual_meaning: a young lad / boy-helper, sometimes dismissive
note: Tamil 'pasanga/payyan'; meaning transfers, the slightly dismissive tone is context.

actual_meaning: a girl / lass, rural North-Indian flavour
note: Region-flavoured Hindi; Tamil 'ponnu'. Meaning maps, the Haryanvi/rustic colour is local.

actual_meaning: a boy / lad, rural North-Indian flavour
note: Rustic Hindi counterpart of chhori; Tamil 'payyan'.

actual_meaning: a girl / young woman; also 'prospective bride'
note: Tamil everyday term; the marriage-market 'ponnu paakka' sense is culturally loaded but the base word maps to Hindi 'ladki'.

actual_meaning: a boy / young man; also 'prospective groom'
note: Tamil counterpart of Hindi 'ladka'; base word transfers, marriage-context flavour is local.

actual_meaning: an attractive person, esp. a girlfriend/crush (Tamil-Eng & pan-Indian slang)
note: Indian-English/Tamil slang ('avan figure') for a romantic interest; objectifying tone, must be re-localised, not literally rendered.

actual_meaning: a person one is romantically infatuated with
note: Global English loanword used as-is in Hindi and Tamil; transparent.

actual_meaning: what's going on / the situation; also a romantic prospect ('koi scene hai?')
note: Indian-English slang with situational and romantic senses; Tamil 'enna scene' mirrors it, but tone is context-bound.

actual_meaning: a flashy, vampish or showy woman (derogatory)
note: Crude North-Indian slang; no Tamil cognate, must be localised to a Tamil equivalent insult.

actual_meaning: a gossip / backbiter who tattles
note: Tamil 'kondi/koonthal' speech-types; meaning transfers, the exact slur differs.

actual_meaning: a stingy, tight-fisted person
note: Tamil 'kanji/lubban'; universal 'miser' concept, fully conveyable.

actual_meaning: a scoundrel / cunning rascal (insult, sometimes affectionate)
note: Urdu swear with a sly-affectionate range; Tamil has its own oaths, so match register not word.

actual_meaning: a rascal / mischief-maker; rogue (often playful)
note: Tamil 'porukki/saettai'; the playful-rogue tone transfers reasonably.

actual_meaning: a too-clever-by-half smart aleck ('over-shaana')
note: The ironic 'wise guy' usage ('zyada shaana mat ban') is idiomatic; Tamil 'over-smart' captures it differently.

actual_meaning: a cunning, sly, opportunistic person
note: 'Switched-on/operational' literal flips to 'sly' in slang; Tamil 'sevuru/over' differ, so adapt.

actual_meaning: a naive, simple, guileless person
note: Reduplicative Hindi idiom; Tamil 'summa irukkura aalu/innocent'. Meaning maps, the rhyme is lost.

actual_meaning: a drama queen / overly theatrical person; melodramatic behaviour
note: Refers to a North-Indian theatre tradition repurposed as 'drama'; Tamil 'over-acting/drama' conveys it without the cultural reference.

actual_meaning: a foreigner/white person; sometimes 'idle/clueless fellow' jokingly
note: Tamil term; Hindi 'gora'. Literal sense maps; the jokey 'do-nothing' extension is Tamil-specific.

actual_meaning: a white person / foreigner
note: Hindi term; Tamil 'vellaikaaran'. Straightforward cross-language map.

actual_meaning: a naive, dim or pampered young man (often mocking)
note: Pet name turned political/youth mockery; no Tamil equivalent, must be re-localised.

actual_meaning: the boys / the lads (one's male crew), casual
note: Plural of 'launda'; Tamil 'pasanga'. Casual 'the boys' sense maps.

actual_meaning: the gang of kids/young lads; one's crew
note: Tamil counterpart of Hindi 'launde/bachche'; transfers across the two.

actual_meaning: the boss / the GOAT; admiring address to a leader or hero (also Rajinikanth)
note: Tamil 'leader' loaded with superstar-fandom connotation; Hindi 'bhai/boss' approximate but the cult flavour is Tamil-specific.
